ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general corn food product entry; no specific entry for grilled corn on skewer. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked maize/corn; no specific skewer-grilled variant code exists. Adapted match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grilled corn skewer as a food product; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard skewer of grilled corn kernels weighing approximately 80 g, as commonly served at Singapore street food stalls and hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Corn, sweet, yellow, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (FDC ID 169998) adapted for grilled/seasoned preparation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked sweet corn used as the primary nutrient anchor. Sodium value elevated to 187.5 mg/100g to reflect typical street-food seasoning (salt added during grilling), consistent with the input record and plausible for a seasoned grilled preparation. All other macros cross-referenced against HPB Singapore Foodo corn entries. Fat slightly elevated from plain boiled corn (1.2 g/100g) to 3.75 g/100g to account for oil applied during grilling — documented as adapted estimate.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweet corn (boiled). Grilled corn on skewer is treated as an analogous preparation; GI is not expected to differ materially from boiled sweet corn. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × (carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = 52 × (22.5 × 0.80) / 100 = 52 × 18.0 / 100 = 9.36, rounded to 9.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 187.5 mg/100g from seasoning; potassium ~270 mg/100g partially offsets sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion to one skewer (80 g, ~150 mg sodium) and request minimal added salt during grilling to keep total sodium within the 2,000 mg/day target; the naturally occurring potassium in corn supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI of 52 and carbohydrate content of 22.5 g/100g; dietary fibre at 3.13 g/100g moderates postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume one skewer (80 g, ~18 g carbohydrate, GL ~9.4) as a moderate-GI snack; pairing with a protein source and avoiding sugar-based glazes will further blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.63 g/100g), zero trans fat, and meaningful dietary fibre (3.13 g/100g) support a heart-healthy profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled corn on skewer is a low-saturated-fat, trans-fat-free snack with fibre that supports LDL cholesterol management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose preparations with minimal added oil and salt to maintain the favourable lipid and sodium profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Corn is a nightshade-adjacent starchy vegetable; chilli powder seasoning is a nightshade-derived spice that may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade-derived spices should request cumin-seasoned or plain grilled corn and moderate portion size; the low saturated fat and vitamin C content are otherwise supportive of anti-inflammatory dietary patterns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Corn on Skewer (烤玉米串) is a popular street food snack in Singapore and across Chinese-influenced Southeast Asian food culture. It consists of sweet corn kernels threaded onto a bamboo or metal skewer and grilled or roasted over an open flame or charcoal grill, typically seasoned with salt, oil, and optionally chilli powder or cumin. The result is a lightly charred, smoky, and subtly sweet snack with a tender yet slightly firm kernel texture. Each skewer weighs approximately 80 g and provides around 100 kcal, 18 g of carbohydrate, 3.75 g of protein, and 3.75 g of fat, with 3.13 g of dietary fibre and 187.5 mg of sodium per 100 g. The glycemic index is approximately 52 (low-moderate), making it a relatively moderate-GI snack when consumed in a single-skewer portion. Clinically, the fibre content supports glycaemic moderation and gut health, while the low saturated fat and zero trans fat profile are favourable for cardiovascular health. Sodium is elevated compared to plain corn due to seasoning and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension. This dish is vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free, and is commonly found at hawker centres, night markets, and Chinese street food stalls throughout Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a corn skewer?
Is corn on a stick gluten free?
Is corn skewer suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cooked sweet corn with upward adjustment for added oil and salt during grilling. No exact grilled-corn-on-skewer entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA as a distinct food item. The adaptation is well-documented and defensible but not an exact source match, warranting medium confidence.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for grilled seasoned corn on skewer is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient detail for this specific grilled preparation is not reported in the primary adapted source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — corn and plant-based oils contain negligible industrial trans fats; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — corn is a plant food containing no dietary cholesterol). potassium_mg of 270.0 mg/100g is an estimate based on USDA FoodData Central cooked sweet corn (~270 mg/100g) and is flagged as an estimate. Ontology: FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03301710 is the closest defensible match for corn as a food product; FoodEx2 A00LC is the closest defensible code for cooked maize. No SNOMED CT code assigned — ontology honesty maintained. GI of 52 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for boiled sweet corn; applied as analogous estimate for grilled preparation.
